content.problemStatement:
For an array AAA of length NNN, let F(A)F(A)F(A) denote the sum of the product of all the subarrays of AAA. Formally,

F(A)=∑L=1N∑R=LN(∏i=LRAi)F(A) = \sum_{L=1}^N \sum_{R=L}^N \left (\prod_{i=L}^R A_i\right )F(A)=L=1∑N​R=L∑N​(i=L∏R​Ai​)For example, let A=[1,0,1]A = [1, 0, 1]A=[1,0,1], then there are 666 possible subarrays:


Subarray [1,1][1, 1][1,1] has product =1= 1=1
Subarray [1,2][1, 2][1,2] has product =0= 0=0
Subarray [1,3][1, 3][1,3] has product =0= 0=0
Subarray [2,2][2, 2][2,2] has product =0= 0=0
Subarray [2,3][2, 3][2,3] has product =0= 0=0
Subarray [3,3][3, 3][3,3] has product =1= 1=1
So F(A)=1+1=2F(A) = 1+1 = 2F(A)=1+1=2.

Given a **binary** array AAA, determine the sum of F(A)F(A)F(A) over all the N!N!N! orderings of AAA modulo 998244353998244353998244353.

Note that orderings here are defined in terms of indices, not elements; which is why every array of length NNN has N!N!N! orderings. For example, the 3!=63! = 63!=6 orderings of A=[1,0,1]A = [1, 0, 1]A=[1,0,1] are:


[1,0,1][1, 0, 1][1,0,1] corresponding to indices [1,2,3][1, 2, 3][1,2,3]
[1,1,0][1, 1, 0][1,1,0] corresponding to indices [1,3,2][1, 3, 2][1,3,2]
[0,1,1][0, 1, 1][0,1,1] corresponding to indices [2,1,3][2, 1, 3][2,1,3]
[0,1,1][0, 1, 1][0,1,1] corresponding to indices [2,3,1][2, 3, 1][2,3,1]
[1,1,0][1, 1, 0][1,1,0] corresponding to indices [3,1,2][3, 1, 2][3,1,2]
[1,0,1][1, 0, 1][1,0,1] corresponding to indices [3,2,1][3, 2, 1][3,2,1]

Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN denoting the le
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers denoting the array AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, output the sum of F(A)F(A)F(A) over all the N!N!N! orderings of AAA, modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.
